title_en: Al-Isabah: Identifying the Companions of the Prophet
content_en:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah by Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani (773–852 AH / 1372–1449 CE) is the definitive classical reference work for identifying the companions of the Prophet Muhammad. The word isabah in the title means hitting the mark or achieving accuracy, reflecting Ibn Hajar's aspiration to provide precise identification where earlier works had sometimes been imprecise or incomplete.

The work addresses a question of fundamental importance in Islamic scholarship: who exactly were the companions (sahabah) of the Prophet? The answer matters because the companion generation has a unique status in Islamic theology and law. The Quran and the Sunnah were transmitted to subsequent generations primarily through the companions. Their collective testimony to the Prophet's teaching constitutes the foundation of Islamic knowledge. Their individual rulings and practices are given weight in Islamic jurisprudence as evidence of how the early community understood and applied the prophetic teaching.

For these reasons, the boundaries of the companion category are not merely a historical question but a theological and legal one. If someone who is actually not a companion is counted as one, their testimonies receive unwarranted authority. If a genuine companion is excluded from the category, their testimonies may be treated with unwarranted suspicion. Precise identification of the companions is therefore a matter of genuine consequence for the reliability of the entire Islamic scholarly tradition.

Ibn Hajar brought the full resources of his mastery of the hadith sciences to this task. He was at the end of a long scholarly tradition of companion biography — from Ibn Sa'd and Ibn Abd al-Barr through Ibn al-Athir — and he was able to synthesize and evaluate everything that had been done before while adding his own extensive original research. The result was a work that the scholarly community immediately recognized as superseding its predecessors and that has never been superseded itself.

This entry treats Al-Isabah as a distinct entry from the fuller treatment under al-isabah-fi-tamyiz-as-sahabah, focusing on the work's place in the broader tradition of defining and documenting the companion generation.
content_id:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah karya Ibnu Hajar al-Asqalani (773-852 H / 1372-1449 M) adalah karya referensi klasik definitif untuk mengidentifikasi para sahabat Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Kata isabah dalam judul berarti mengenai sasaran atau mencapai ketepatan, mencerminkan aspirasi Ibnu Hajar untuk memberikan identifikasi yang tepat di mana karya-karya sebelumnya terkadang tidak tepat atau tidak lengkap.

Karya ini membahas pertanyaan yang sangat penting dalam ilmu Islam: siapa sebenarnya para sahabat (sahabah) Nabi? Jawabannya penting karena generasi sahabat memiliki status yang unik dalam teologi dan hukum Islam. Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah diwariskan kepada generasi berikutnya terutama melalui para sahabat. Kesaksian kolektif mereka terhadap pengajaran Nabi ﷺ merupakan fondasi pengetahuan Islam.

Karena alasan-alasan ini, batas-batas kategori sahabat bukan sekadar pertanyaan historis tetapi juga teologis dan hukum. Jika seseorang yang sebenarnya bukan sahabat dihitung sebagai sahabat, kesaksian mereka mendapat otoritas yang tidak semestinya. Identifikasi yang tepat tentang para sahabat adalah masalah konsekuensi nyata bagi keandalan seluruh tradisi keilmuan Islam.

Ibnu Hajar membawa semua sumber daya dari penguasaannya atas ilmu hadis untuk tugas ini. Ia berada di ujung tradisi ilmiah yang panjang tentang biografi sahabat — dari Ibnu Sa'd dan Ibnu Abd al-Barr hingga Ibnu al-Atsir — dan ia mampu mensintesis dan mengevaluasi semua yang telah dilakukan sebelumnya sambil menambahkan penelitian orisinalnya yang ekstensif. Hasilnya adalah sebuah karya yang komunitas ilmiah segera mengakuinya sebagai melampaui para pendahulunya dan yang belum pernah dilampaui sendiri.
content_ar:
Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahabah by Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani (773–852 AH / 1372–1449 CE) is the definitive classical reference work for identifying the companions of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم Muhammad. The word isabah in the title means hitting the mark or achieving accuracy, reflecting Ibn Hajar's aspiration to provide precise identification where earlier works had sometimes been imprecise or incomplete.

The work addresses a question of fundamental importance in Islamic scholarship: who exactly were the companions (sahabah) of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم? The answer matters because the companion generation has a unique status in Islamic theology and law. The Quran and the Sunnah were transmitted to subsequent generations primarily through the companions. Their collective testimony to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم's teaching constitutes the foundation of Islamic knowledge. Their individual rulings and practices are given weight in Islamic jurisprudence as evidence of how the early community understood and applied the prophetic teaching.

For these reasons, the boundaries of the companion category are not merely a historical question but a theological and legal one. If someone who is actually not a companion is counted as one, their testimonies receive unwarranted authority. If a genuine companion is excluded from the category, their testimonies may be treated with unwarranted suspicion. Precise identification of the companions is therefore a matter of genuine consequence for the reliability of the entire Islamic scholarly tradition.

Ibn Hajar brought the full resources of his mastery of the hadith sciences to this task. He was at the end of a long scholarly tradition of companion biography — from Ibn Sa'd and Ibn Abd al-Barr through Ibn al-Athir — and he was able to synthesize and evaluate everything that had been done before while adding his own extensive original research. The result was a work that the scholarly community immediately recognized as superseding its predecessors and that has never been superseded itself.

This entry treats Al-Isabah as a distinct entry from the fuller treatment under al-isabah-fi-tamyiz-as-sahabah, focusing on the work's place in the broader tradition of defining and documenting the companion generation.
